title: Dark Horse to translate Shinji Ikari Raising Project
display_title: Dark Horse to translate Shinji Ikari Raising Project
wikitext:
[]: Dark Horse to translate Shinji Ikari Raising Project             Quote Post by V » Fri Jul 04, 2008 4:34 pm http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2008-07-04/dark-horse-gets-eva-shinji-ikari-raising-project-manga Dark Horse is going to produce and release the English translation of "Shinji Ikari Raising Project", that is, Osamu Takahashi's manga based on the video game spinoff

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 04, 2008 4:41 pm I would be more happy that ISRP manga is being translated if it had an actual plot in later chapters instead of just...well, fan service. (As well as actual Rei instead of RiAO). That's awesome news though. Thanks, V!

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Jul 05, 2008 11:31 am you're a fan of this? Fanservice at the end? Meaning what exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jul 05, 2008 12:43 pm I've been reading some chapters of it but it could be better executed with things. V wrote: Fanservice at the end? Meaning what exactly?

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sat Jul 05, 2008 1:58 pm "In the last chapters"? You mean in those chapters since chapter... 1?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Jul 06, 2008 12:43 am Yay! I think I'm gonna be banned from the AT-Translations board over this actually though... Not only did I suck as an editor for them, I also kinda stated that everyone who doesn't buy it, or is unhappy at this news, is a thief and a douchebag. Also, Dark Horse will get this out much faster than AT-T was anyway, lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Sun Jul 06, 2008 6:15 pm The spanish translation is already out in Spain. I've just started reading it. At least it looks way better than the dreadful "Angelic Days".

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Jul 08, 2008 1:57 am I thought it would be interesting to release Ikari Shinji Ikusei Keikaku . I would like it to be a fun book, and specifically, I'd like it to be a venue for Evangelion fan art and letters the way "Misato's Fan Service Center" was back in the day. --C.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jul 08, 2008 12:18 pm Carl Horn wrote: I thought it would be interesting to release Ikari Shinji Ikusei Keikaku . I would like it to be a fun book, and specifically, I'd like it to be a venue for Evangelion fan art and letters the way "Misato's Fan Service Center" was back in the day. --C.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 08, 2008 1:47 pm Carl Horn wrote: a venue for Evangelion fan art and letters

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Jul 08, 2008 1:53 pm Different companies, different books, same editor --C.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jul 08, 2008 2:15 pm Carl Horn wrote: I'd like it to be a venue for Evangelion fan art and letters the way "Misato's Fan Service Center" was back in the day.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Jul 08, 2008 2:43 pm Sure! Maybe I'm being a little premature in bringing all this up, since Vol. 1 won't appear in stores until late spring of next year, but we'll probably start production on it before long. I'll try to set up a dedicated e-mail address soon where people can send in their fan art and letters. To paraphrase Abraham Simpson, "We did it all the time in the '90s!" --C.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Tue Jul 08, 2008 2:46 pm Is this going to be right-to-left or left-to-right?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Jul 08, 2008 3:55 pm BobBQ wrote: Is this going to be right-to-left or left-to-right?

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Jul 08, 2008 4:07 pm Right-to-left, although it would be fun to experiment with alternate formats, like printing it on a harisen you could open up and then whack people with, or deliberately not gluing the spine, so the pages fall randomly to the ground and you cast your future from the pattern. --C.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jul 08, 2008 4:07 pm Carl Horn wrote: Sure! Maybe I'm being a little premature in bringing all this up, since Vol. 1 won't appear in stores until late spring of next year, but we'll probably start production on it before long. I'll try to set up a dedicated e-mail address soon where people can send in their fan art and letters. To paraphrase Abraham Simpson, "We did it all the time in the '90s!" --C.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Tue Jul 08, 2008 5:14 pm V wrote: Carl Horn wrote: Sure! Maybe I'm being a little premature in bringing all this up, since Vol. 1 won't appear in stores until late spring of next year, but we'll probably start production on it before long. I'll try to set up a dedicated e-mail address soon where people can send in their fan art and letters. To paraphrase Abraham Simpson, "We did it all the time in the '90s!" --C.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jul 08, 2008 5:21 pm V wrote: "this is a common thing in many anime series because pretty much nowhere but the USA do they consider 1st degree cousin relationships to be incest"

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jul 08, 2008 5:44 pm Mr. Tines wrote: V wrote: "this is a common thing in many anime series because pretty much nowhere but the USA do they consider 1st degree cousin relationships to be incest"
